Metadata.title: Jhonatan Duran - UX/UI Designer
Metadata.description: Jhonatan Duran's Portfolio - UX/UI Designer with frontend development experience

HomePage.title: Hello, I'm
HomePage.job-desc: I'm a UX/UI Designer with frontend development experience. Passionate about creating meaningful, human-centered digital experiences, I combine creativity with technical understanding to design intuitive interfaces.

case1.descripcion: Comprehensive redesign of a web platform focused on improving usability, optimizing key flows, and reducing user friction through research, prototyping, and validation.

case2.descripcion: Design and prototyping of a mobile application from scratch, covering the entire user flow from login to main actions, with a mobile-first approach.

case3.descripcion: Enhancement of an existing platform through benchmark analysis, user surveys, and interface redesign to simplify information and increase usage efficiency.

Footer.title: CONTACT
Footer.subtitle: Let's connect
Footer.description: Get in touch to chat or simply to say hello! 👋

Case1.tag: Case Study: Creating a Web/Mobile Application
Case1.desc: TokinAi is an application designed to help entrepreneurs, content creators, and marketing teams generate visual and textual content for social media quickly and efficiently using AI.
Case1.desc2: The project emerged from the need to democratize professional content creation for small businesses and independent creators who don't have dedicated design teams.
Case1.section1-desc: Many entrepreneurs face difficulties maintaining an active and consistent presence on social media.
Case1.section1-desc2: Small entrepreneurs spend between 5 and 8 hours per week creating content, time that could be invested in strategic activities for business growth. Additionally, 73% of surveyed users expressed feeling overwhelmed by the need to consistently produce quality content across multiple platforms, mainly due to lack of time, resources, or specialized knowledge.
Case1.section1-desc3: This situation creates a significant barrier to digital visibility and sustained growth of their brands.
Case1.section2-desc: Similar platforms were examined to understand the market and differentiation opportunities.
Case1.pippit-desc: Pippit is a platform with AI-powered automatic multimedia content creation features, focused on generating videos and images for marketing and social media.
Case1.fortaleza-1: AI for automatic video and image generation
Case1.debilidad-2: Does not suggest posting frequency for different networks
Case1.survey-desc: To ensure our product truly met our users' needs, it was essential to conduct interviews with new and established entrepreneurs. These insights shaped our strategy, allowing us to create a product that delivered real value and addressed their specific pain points.
Case1.survey-item-2: Lack of knowledge in digital marketing
Case1.survey-item-3: Have not used AI tools to create content
Case1.survey-item-4: Do not have a website or social media
Case1.survey-item-6: Do not measure results or metrics
Case1.survey-item-7: Do not know how to run ads (advertising)
Case1.insights-desc: After conducting user interviews, contextual research, analysis of similar platforms, and analyzing the collected data, I was able to classify the acquired insights into these three categories:
Case1.insight-time-des: Most entrepreneurs lack the time to learn, execute, and revitalize their social media accounts.
Case1.settings-desc: Many users find that AI-generated content is generic and not personalized or aligned with their brand.
Case1.unknown-desc: Many entrepreneurs know they have a real business but lack knowledge about what, when, or how to post.
Case1.user-person-desc: Based on the research, we defined three user profiles that represent the spectrum of TokinAi's needs.
Case1.user-1-comment: I know I need to post more, but between running my business and everything else, I just don't have the time.
Case1.user-1-objetive-1: Gain visibility without spending hours on social media
Case1.user-1-objetive-2: Attract new customers with professional content
Case1.user-1-frustration-1: Doesn't know what to post or when
Case1.user-1-frustration-2: Fears looking unprofessional on social media
Case1.user-2-comment: I manage multiple brands at once and I always need fresh ideas. It's exhausting to reinvent yourself every week.
Case1.user-2-objetive-1: Generate content ideas for multiple clients
Case1.user-2-objetive-2: Maintain each brand's voice and consistency
Case1.user-2-frustrations-1: Creative burnout from managing multiple accounts
Case1.user-2-frustrations-2: Generic tools that don't respect brand tone
Case1.user-3-comment: I have the ideas, but I need help transforming them into posts that truly connect.
Case1.user-3-objetive-2: Monetize her personal brand with valuable content
Case1.user-3-frustrations-1: Doesn't know how to adapt content to different platforms
Case1.user-3-frustrations-2: Low consistency due to lack of structure
Case1.design-desc: With a better understanding of user needs, I dove into creating wireframes and iterations, focusing on:
Case1.custom-settings-desc: For this aspect, a progressive 3-step onboarding flow was designed for first-time users to provide their business data and competitor information. This enables the generation of personalized content aligned with the brand.
Case1.custom-step-1-desc: In this step, the user had to log in with their social media accounts to obtain their business data such as business name, followers, posts, etc.
Case1.custom-step-2-desc: In this step, the user had to enter their competitors' URLs so the AI could analyze and gather information about their competition.
Case1.custom-step-3: Review and edit the inferred data
Case1.custom-step-3-desc: In this step, the user could review and edit the data obtained from their business and their competitors. This data was gathered from their social media accounts and their competitors' URLs. Some information, such as the tone of their posts, etc., was inferred by the AI.
Case1.findings-desc: The following issues were detected in the initial tests:
Case1.findings-1: The onboarding flow was too lengthy. Users abandoned the process before configuring their business profile, preventing them from completing onboarding and therefore accessing the platform's main functionality.
Case1.findings-2: Due to the platform's target audience—business owners with limited time and basic knowledge—many didn't have social media accounts, so their business data couldn't be obtained.
Case1.findings-3: Other users who did have social media accounts weren't logged into them, so they had to search for or remember their credentials to sign in. This was time-consuming and many didn't follow through.
Case1.findings-4: Others didn't have enough content on their social media, so the AI couldn't gather sufficient data to create a brand profile.
Case1.findings-5: When identifying competitors, users didn't know what to enter in the competitor URL field. This caused confusion and they didn't know what to input.
Case1.solution-proposal-desc: User feedback was taken into account and the first step of onboarding was redesigned. The approach shifted away from requiring users to log in with their social media accounts, and instead asked them to enter their business URL or their competitor's URL. This way, data could be obtained from their business and their competitors.
Case1.problems-findings-desc: This change was not well received by users, as the field for their business URL and their competitor's URL caused confusion. Additionally, we returned to the problem of it being a cumbersome and lengthy process. Having to search for their business URL and their competitor's URL, many users didn't do it.
Case1.final-solution-desc: Another solution was proposed, this time we opted to include a page search feature, so users could search for their business page and add it easily. This solution was well received by users, as it allowed them to obtain their business data quickly and efficiently. Without having to leave the platform to get their business information. Additionally, the onboarding was reduced to 2 steps, so users could obtain their business data more quickly, and the competitors section was added as another data point to review inferred by AI.
Case1.to-review-desc: Although this solution reduced friction and allowed many users to progress through onboarding, a problem arose where some pages were not found in the search, so users had to enter their business data manually with the URL. However, this solution was considered the best option, due to the rate of users who progressed compared to other methods.
Case1.time-iteration-desc: For the time problem, a solution was proposed that would allow users to generate content quickly and efficiently. A strategy system was designed that allows users to create multiple posts in a single process.
Case1.strategy-creation-desc: Here the user can determine their strategy's objective, the type of content they want to generate, and the tone they wish to use. By default, a strategy with ready-to-use variants is proposed.
Case1.content-review-desc: Here the user can review what content will be generated (Image, video, text), which social network it will be published on, and the credit value required. They can modify it if necessary.
Case1.calendar-desc: The system generates and schedules content and recommends the best times for each social network. The user can modify it at any time.
Case1.findings-time-1: Users only edited the objective; they didn't interact with the content editing options
Case1.findings-time-2: Most users accepted the AI-proposed content, so it was decided to simplify the flow.
Case1.findings-time-3: The content review modal contained too much information and was confusing for users.
Case1.solution-proposal-time-desc: The content strategy creation flow was redesigned to address the findings:
Case1.solution-proposal-time-1: The content strategy creation flow was simplified, presenting the user with a single input where they could enter the strategy's objective, which was the field users edited most.
Case1.solution-proposal-time-2: Fields for content tone, hashtags, or publication date were placed with less hierarchy to avoid overwhelming the user.
Case1.solution-proposal-time-3: The content review modal was eliminated, replacing it with a content strategy summary with information better separated and organized.
Case1.to-review-time: In this improvement, although the flow was more direct, it was noted that users still weren't interacting with the content customization fields, so it was suggested to review how to improve the content personalization experience.
Case1.testing-desc: I tested the product at various stages of the project.
Case1.pruebas-beta-desc: Before launching the app, we tested it with a group of entrepreneurs. This helped us obtain some indicators and identify friction points
Case1.pruebas-a-b-desc: Several small flows in the tool were tested to measure how users interacted with a given flow and whether it was intuitive for the user
Case1.content-fast-desc: Users highlighted the ability to generate content efficiently as a major time saver.
Case1.automatic-posted-desc: Users were excited about the content recommendations for posting and suggested times for different social networks.
Case1.init-social-networks-desc: Many users didn't have their accounts open in the browser, which created friction in the process. This caused users to drop off at this step.
Case1.learnings-1-desc: During the initial interviews, most of our users were between 40-65 years old, with limited knowledge of marketing, brand growth, AI, and technology in general. This indicated that interactions that might work well for a tech-savvy audience familiar with trends might not work as effectively for this age range.
Case1.learnings-2-desc: Project timelines were tight, which didn't allow for all the processes that should theoretically be carried out in a UX/UI project. However, we tried to balance the process to ensure that deliverables maintained a user-centered design at each stage.
Case1.learnings-3-desc: Continuously monitor user usage and gather feedback for improvement. It greatly helped identify friction points for users and enabled modifications and, at times, complete redesigns.
Case1.learnings-4-desc: at different stages is crucial to validate ideas and designs, both with stakeholders and the development team, to maintain communication, stay aligned, and avoid delays due to lack of communication

Case2.desc: Social Alert is a social media listening and monitoring tool for crisis management and early warning. It identifies negative or positive comments, words, and patterns to prevent crises or act in time.
Case2.desc-2: The project requires a complete redesign of the web application to align with new business objectives.
Case2.problem-desc: The Social Alert Platform presented various usability issues that directly affected user experience and product efficiency.
Case2.problem-2: Users couldn't clearly find where information was located.
Case2.problem-5: Limited agility and reliability of the platform for users.
Case2.problem-6: Lack of clarity in information hierarchy.
Case2.problem-7: Competition from more modern and efficient tools.
Case2.problem-desc-1: These issues negatively impacted user satisfaction and the platform's effective value.
Case2.objetives-1: Improve overall usability of the proposed flow.
Case2.objetives-3: Design a consistent experience across web and mobile.
Case2.user-research: To better understand users and the product context, the following activities were conducted:
Case2.research-3: User navigation research using tools like Clarity and Posthog
Case2.surveys-desc: Both active users and the internal team were surveyed. The information gathered from this process was as follows:
Case2.table-row-1-col-2: High difficulty using AND / OR and exclusions
Case2.table-row-1-col-3: Main source of errors and tickets
Case2.table-row-1-col-4: Visual logic editor, clear examples, and validations
Case2.table-row-2-col-2: Medium or low confidence; users manually review results
Case2.table-row-2-col-3: Confusing words and noise in results
Case2.table-row-3-col-2: Functional but long, repetitive process with excessive scrolling
Case2.table-row-4-col-2: Construction of long and complex queries
Case2.table-row-4-col-3: Tedious to add and edit terms
Case2.table-row-5-col-4: Improvement of visual hierarchy and readability
Case2.table-row-6-col-2: Many options exist but are rarely used
Case2.table-row-6-col-4: Simplify options or show contextual help
Case2.table-row-7-col-4: Integrate alerts directly into the creation flow
Case2.table-row-8-col-2: Most tickets come from query creation
Case2.table-row-8-col-3: Users don't see data due to misconfiguration
Case2.benchmark-desc: The following platforms that perform social media monitoring or listening were analyzed to discover improvement opportunities and differentiators
Case2.heuristic-desc: A heuristic analysis of the platform was conducted to identify certain usability issues.
Case2.ui: The following usability issues were identified on the platform:
Case2.heatmap-desc: It was proposed to integrate user tracking tools like Clarity and Posthog to better understand user behavior on the platform and thus identify usability issues, heat maps, where they spend more time, etc.
Case2.heatmap-query-desc: The data shows that users spend a lot of time on query creation, indicating that they have trouble creating queries.
Case2.heat-dash: Here it is observed that users frequently use the query search area, and the first (Live) and second tab (What) on the dashboard, while the other tabs are seldom or never used.
Case2.insght-desc: With the information gathered from the surveys and benchmark, the following insights were identified:
Case2.insight-1-desc: Friction was found in important flows such as:
Case2.insight-focus-1: Focus: Simplify the most important flows.
Case2.insight-2-desc: A lot of scattered and repeated information on the main dashboard, which made it difficult to read and understand the information.
Case2.insight-2-focus: Focus: Group and simplify the information.
Case2.insight-3-desc: There are usability issues on the platform that hinder the user experience.
Case2.insight-3-focus: Focus: Improve the platform's usability by correcting the points identified in the heuristic analysis.
Case2.design-desc: We worked on improving the most important flows, such as the query creation, dashboard, and alerts flow.
Case2.create-query-desc: The query creation flow was redesigned to make it simpler and faster for the user. The flow was divided into 2 steps.
Case2.input-desc: To facilitate the query creation process, a query input was included so users can create queries in natural language and have them translated behind the scenes into complex queries (AND/OR).
Case2.to-improve-1-1: By having a natural language query input, the user doesn't need to understand AND/OR, they only need to write what they want and the system takes care of translating it into complex queries.
Case2.verify-query-desc: In this section, the user can see the query that has been built in different formats and can edit it, add keywords, or remove keywords.
Case2.problem-solving-1-desc: Taking into account the difficulties and problems users had with the previous query format, the query is displayed in different formats so users can better understand how it's being constructed.
Case2.problem-solving-2-desc: Users can edit the query, add keywords, or remove keywords as needed on a single screen, eliminating the need to scroll.
Case2.problem-solving-3-desc: Visual aids, hints, and tooltips were implemented so users can better understand how the query is being built, what each query block does, and how it relates to other parts.
Case2.problem-solving-4-desc: A small sample of posts found with the current query now appears, allowing users to see the query results in real time. If it matches what they're looking for, they can proceed to execute the full query.
Case2.tablero-desc: For the dashboard, we chose to reduce the amount of unnecessary and repetitive information and display only relevant information, properly grouped for the user.
Case2.tablero-new-desc: The dashboard was reorganized so users can view information clearly and in an orderly manner, with the most frequently used filters—based on collected metrics—positioned at the top for easy access.
Case2.to-improve-2-desc: The previous dashboard displayed too much information, making it difficult for users to find what they needed. The number of tabs was reduced and information was reorganized more clearly.
Case2.to-improve-3-desc: Based on observations and heat maps that reflected where users spent the most time, the most frequently used filters were positioned at the top for easy access.
Case2.to-improve-4-desc: The heuristic analysis identified several areas for improvement, which were implemented in the dashboard redesign. This included information hierarchy, component standardization, icons, minimalist design, etc.
Case2.alert-settings-desc: The alert creation flow was separated from the query configuration section, allowing users to configure alerts independently without mixing the two processes.
Case2.to-improve-alerts-1-desc: The alert creation flow was separated from the query configuration section, allowing users to configure alerts independently without mixing the two processes.
Case2.to-improve-alerts-2-desc: The alert configuration form was redesigned to be more user-friendly and easier to use. Unnecessary fields were removed and tooltips were added to explain each field.
Case2.to-improve-alerts-3-desc: Testing was conducted with internal users to validate the redesign of the alert configuration form. Positive feedback was received from users, who indicated that the form was more user-friendly and easier to use.
Case2.testing-desc: Before code implementation, validation sessions were conducted:
Case2.testing-desc-3: Adjustments based on feedback prior to development.
Case2.final-result: The result was a new version of the product with:
Case2.final-result-4: Consistent design aligned with a visual system.
Case2.learning-1-desc: Some platforms had already detected and corrected many of the errors that ours had, so we replicated those solutions, always adapting them to our own needs and objectives.
Case2.learning-2-desc: Many design decisions were made based on information gathered from users and the team, since placing certain information in a specific location was essential to streamline their work and navigation on the platform
Case2.learning-3-desc: Involving all stakeholders at different stages is crucial to validate ideas and designs, both with stakeholders and with the development team, to maintain good communication and avoid problems during development.

Case3.desc: Scan Audience is an advanced tool designed to evaluate your brand's performance and make comparisons with other brands in the market.
Case3.desc-1: The platform presented friction at important points, failing to clearly communicate the product's value or guide the user toward the main actions.
Case3.problems-desc: Based on internal feedback and usage observation, the following problems were identified:
Case3.problem-1: The login didn't clearly communicate the platform's purpose
Case3.problem-2: The Home didn't present useful information
Case3.problem-3: Users took time to identify the main action
Case3.problem-4: There was little visual hierarchy and cognitive overload
Case3.problem-desc: These factors affected the user experience and product adoption.
Case3.objetive-bussines-1: Attract new customers and increase brand awareness
Case3.research-desc: Light research was conducted, focused solely on the screens to be redesigned:
Case3.research-4: User navigation research using tools like Clarity and Posthog
Case3.user-voice-1: I love the depth of the data, but sometimes I get lost among so many tabs and information. There's too much information and I don't know where to start.
Case3.user-voice-2: The information is very valuable, but the interface looks oversaturated with colors and disorganized. Hard to read.
Case3.user-voice-3: I would like to be able to create my own reports with the information I need. Also download the reports in different formats, not just PDF.
Case3.scene-desc: Valeria needs to create a space to compare her brand's performance against 3 key competitors to present it at a strategy meeting in 20 minutes.
Case3.accion-1: Enters the platform and looks for the "Spaces" section.
Case3.action-2: Must select from the pages she has entered, if there are none she must add pages. This redirects her to the "Pages" section, exiting the space creation process.
Case3.action-3: Adds the pages required for her analysis and returns to space creation.
Case3.action-4: Takes a first look at the analytics dashboard.
Case3.pensamiento-1: "I hope this is a quick and simple comparison."
Case3.pensamiento-2: "Why can't I add pages from space creation?"
Case3.pensamiento-4: "What information does this Dashboard provide me? How can I compare with competitors?"
Case3.pain-point-1: There is no clear flow for creating spaces.
Case3.pain-point-2: It's not possible to add pages from space creation. You need to exit space creation to add pages.
Case3.pain-point-3: Not intuitive how to add pages
Case3.pain-point-4: The initial dashboard has too much information.
Case3.phase-1-hmw: How might we provide direct access to space creation?
Case3.phase-2-hmw: How might we simplify space creation and the add pages flow?
Case3.phase-4-hmw: How might we synthesize the dashboard information and make it more intuitive?
Case3.scene-desc-1: Jimmy needs to compare his brand's performance against a specific competitor in the last quarter.
Case3.action-1: Look for the "Comparison" option in the tabs.
Case3.action-2-1: Look for where to select the pages and the start and end dates.
Case3.action-2-2: Look for the "Apply" button to generate the comparison.
Case3.pensamiento-1-1: "There are so many tabs, where is the comparison?"
Case3.pensamiento-2-2: "How do I select the pages and the start and end dates? I can't find the date picker... Ah, it's hidden in this gray dropdown."
Case3.pensamiento-3-3: "Why aren't the changes being applied? Oh, I have to press the apply button."
Case3.pensamiento-4-4: "Finally... but I had to repeat the process twice for the second column."
Case3.pain-1: Too much information: there are too many tabs on the dashboard
Case3.pain-2: Poor accessibility: the page and date selectors look like plain text, not clickable fields.
Case3.pain-3: No visual hierarchy: the "Apply" button competes with 5 other bright colors.
Case3.pain-4: No bulk action: she has to configure column by column; it's not intuitive.
Case3.phase-2-2-hmw: How might we simplify the configuration for selecting pages and dates?
Case3.phase-3-3-hmw: How might we highlight the 'Apply' button to make it more intuitive?
Case3.phase-4-4-hmw: How might we synthesize the comparison information and make it more intuitive?
Case3.heuristic-analisys-desc: A heuristic analysis of the platform was conducted to identify certain usability issues.
Case3.ui-desc: The following usability issues were identified on the platform:
Case3.design-desc: Work was done on redesigning the platform, applying the learnings obtained from research. The main improvements were:
Case3.home-desc: Two new sections were added to the home screen, so users can have direct access to options they didn't have before.
Case3.funcionalidad-desc: The home screen went from being a screen without functionality to a screen with quick access to the platform's features.
Case3.spaces-desc: Now users can see their spaces on the home screen and can access them directly.
Case3.add-pages-desc: The add pages screen was redesigned, improving usability and user experience.
Case3.input-desc: The add pages input was given more prominence so users can find it more easily.
Case3.paginas-agregadas-desc: Users can now view their added pages in a clearer and more organized way.
Case3.help-desc: Visual aids, hints, tooltips, and descriptions were added to help users better understand each section.
Case3.espacios-desc: Creating spaces was a process that required several steps and wasn't very clear to users. First, users had to add pages, then in a separate process create a space by selecting which pages they wanted to monitor.
Case3.espacios-desc-1: Now they can create a space and add pages in a single process, making the flow simpler and clearer for users.
Case3.agregar-paginas-desc: Users can now add and select pages while creating the space, making the flow simpler and clearer.
Case3.paginas-seleccionadas-desc: Clearly visualize which pages have been selected for the space.
Case3.usabilidad-desc: The usability of the space creation form was improved, helping users better understand each section.
Case3.tablero-desc: The dashboard and comparison view were redesigned, standardizing information, improving the information hierarchy, and maintaining a consistent experience.
Case3.jerarquia-desc: The information hierarchy was improved, helping users better understand each section.
Case3.menos-pestanas-desc: The number of tabs was reduced, helping users better understand each section.
Case3.comparativa-mejorada-desc: The comparison feature was improved, allowing users to compare themselves with other users more clearly and efficiently.
Case3.impact-1: Improved initial understanding of the platform
Case3.impact-2: Reduced time it takes users to identify key actions
Case3.impact-4: Positive feedback from internal users and stakeholders
Case3.learning-1-desc: Maintaining a clear order with visual hierarchy and a clean UI improves the first impression and platform adoption.
Case3.learning-2-desc: Validation and application of heuristics can be the starting point to improve and standardize design. They can be key when designing to know how we want to communicate our designs.
Case3.info-desc: Information hierarchy is key for users to better understand each section. Keeping colors, icons, sizes, typography, etc. consistent helps communicate information clearly and efficiently.

About.service-1: I research, interview, and analyze to make decisions based on real data, not assumptions.
About.service-2: I transform findings into coherent, scalable, and accessible visual interfaces.
About.service-3: I bring designs to code with React and Next.js, ensuring pixel-perfect fidelity.
About.desc-1: Hi, I'm Jona, a UX/UI Designer based in Quito.
About.desc-2: With over 6 years of experience building high-impact digital products. My unique value lies in the balance between two worlds: UX/UI design strategy and software engineering. I don't just design visually appealing interfaces based on deep research; with my background in software development, I understand how code works and how it contributes to superior usability. This 360° vision allows me to ensure that every pixel designed is technically viable and contributes to a superior user experience.
About.desc-3: Throughout my career, I've been able to collaborate remotely, working as a consultant for companies in the U.S. (Aktiun) and leading innovation processes in Ecuador:
About.desc-4: UX/UI Focus: Specialist in translating research findings and A/B testing into high-fidelity prototypes. I'm obsessed with reducing friction in critical flows, ensuring that design always responds to real user needs.
About.desc-5: I firmly believe that successful software is born from the intersection of clean code and intuitive human experience. My goal is to continue leading projects where technical innovation and user-centered design are the pillars for scaling global products.
About.desc-6: Before opening Figma, I understand the problem. I believe the best design is the one you don't notice, because it simply solves.
